name: AccuQuote AgentCloud REST API
description:
REST API for third-party access to the AccuQuote AgentCloud platform.

The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", 
"SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and 
"OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in 
BCP 14 [RFC2119] [RFC8174] when, and only when, they appear in all capitals,
as shown here.

## Authentication
The API authenticates requests using HTTP basic authentication as described 
in [RFC 7617](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7617). Primarily, this means 
that each request MUST include an `Authorization` header with the value 
`Basic <token>`, where `<token>` is the string `<username>:<password>`
encoded in base 64. 

Because basic authentication credentials are not encrypted, and because the 
data may contain sensitive information, the request MUST be made over HTTPS 
using TLS version 1.2 or higher.

HTTP basic authentication is intended for server-to-server communication and 
MUST NOT be used from the client side, where end users and malicious parties 
could potentially gain access to the unencrypted credentials.

## Request Format
Each action or resource in the API is associated with an endpoint and an 
HTTP method. Each of these defines a specific request format that MUST be 
used when making requests to the API. In general, requests with bodies 
(e.g. `POST` or `PUT`) MUST have the content type `application/json` and 
MUST have a body consisting of a single JSON value (typically an object or 
array, as defined by the specification here).

Request URIs are typically of the form `https://aq.elagy.com/api/v1/<resource>`, 
where `<resource>` is the path to the specific resource or action being 
requested.

### Validation
The following are some common requirements for individual fields in the API. 
The requirements MUST be satisfied for a request to validate successfully.

- "MUST NOT be empty." The value of the field MUST NOT be an empty value, 
e.g. an empty string or array. If the field is required, then the key MUST 
be present in the body, and the value MUST NOT be empty. If the field is 
optional, then the key MAY be omitted; however, if it is present, the value 
MUST NOT be empty.
- "Optional." This field MAY be omitted. If the field is present, the value 
MUST satisfy all other requirements. If omitted, other requirements do not 
apply.
- "Required for [source] leads." Certain additional fields MAY be required 
if the lead comes from a particular source, e.g. Homebridge. In those cases, 
the field SHALL be marked "Required for [source] leads," where "[source]" 
will be replaced with the name of the source for which the field is 
required. For leads from other sources, the field MAY be omitted.
- "Valid IPv4 or IPv6 address." If present, the field MUST be a string whose 
contents are a valid Internet Protocol version 4 or 6 address. IPv4 is 
defined in [RFC 791](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc791), and IPv6 is
defined in [RFC 8200](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc791). 
- "Valid email address." If present, the field MUST contain exactly one `@` 
symbol, with at least one character on each side.
- "Valid phone number." The number MUST be valid under the North American 
Numbering Plan (NANP). The first digit of the area code MUST NOT contain 0 
or 1. The first digit of the prefix MUST NOT contain 0 or 1. The area code 
and prefix MUST NOT match the pattern `[2-9]11`. See 
[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/North_American_Numbering_Plan#Modern_plan)
for a detailed explanation of the NANP.
